latest.content:

RNS Number : 7888W
Ascent Resources PLC
16 March 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN THE UNITED STATES, CANADA, JAPAN, SOUTH AFRICA OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL. 16 March 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Grant of Share Options / PDMR Notification
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, announces that it has granted options over a total of 30,000,000 ordinary shares of £0.005 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares"), including 15 million to the Non Executive Chairman and 15 million to a Non Executive Director, under individual share option agreements.
The options have an exercise price of 0.65 pence per Ordinary Share and will vest in three approximately equal tranches on 12 March 2027, 12 March 2028 and 12 March 2029, subject to continued service (or accelerated vesting in the case of a good leaver cessation, including death). Each tranche has an exercise period of five years from its vesting date.
The options are not subject to performance conditions beyond continued service, but have an exercise price at a premium to the current share price which aligns the interests of the directors with those of shareholders as the share price is likely to be a proxy for operational performance.
Notifications have been made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation and are set out below.

latest.title: Update on Slovenia Disputes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1283W
Ascent Resources PLC
11 March 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN THE UNITED STATES, CANADA, JAPAN, SOUTH AFRICA OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL. ANY FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THESE RESTRICTIONS MAY CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF APPLICABLE SECURITIES LAWS. PLEASE SEE THE SECTION ENTITLED "IMPORTANT INFORMATION" TOWARDS THE END OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
11 March 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Update on Slovenia Disputes
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to provide a short update on the Company's legacy Slovenian claims, where recent developments continue to reinforce
Ascent Slovenia Limited's (
ASL) position ahead of expected near
‑
term decisions.
Domestic Disputes
The Slovenian court has fully rejected Geoenergo's challenge to the jurisdiction of the domestic Arbitration Tribunal which found in ASL's (a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company) favour in relation to ASL's claim for approximately €7.8 million in unpaid hydrocarbon production proceeds plus late interest, as announced on 3 July 2025. The court also noted that no other creditor contests ASL's claim in the Geoenergo insolvency estate. Further court decisions are expected in the coming weeks/months (first in relation to Ascent's court filing for recognition of the arbitral award, and second on Geoenergo's lawsuit for the invalidation of the arbitral award). Recovery remains subject to the conclusion of the administration process.
Also as announced on 30 October 2025, ASL continues to defend claims brought by Petrol Geo d.o.o. against the Joint Venture and remains engaged in seeking an amicable resolution of both parties' respective claims.
Energy Charter Treaty Dispute
The Company is aware of no delays to the ICSID process and continues to expect the Tribunal's award on the merits this month. A further update will be provided in due course.
Enquiries
Ascent Resources plc
Jean-Michel Doublet
info3@ascentresources.co.uk
Zeus, Nominated Adviser & Broker
James Joyce / James Bavister
0203 829 5000

28 January 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Grant of Share Options / PDMR Notification
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, announces that it has granted options over a total of 50,000,000 ordinary shares of £0.005 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") to members of the senior management team, including 25,000,000 options to its Chief Executive Officer, under individual share option agreements.
The options have an exercise price of 0.65 pence per Ordinary Share and will vest in three approximately equal tranches on 1 July 2026, 1 July 2027 and 1 July 2028, subject to continued service (or accelerated vesting in the case of a good leaver cessation, including death). Each tranche has an exercise period of five years from its vesting date. The options are not subject to performance conditions beyond continued service. The grant is intended to align the interests of the senior management team with those of shareholders.
The grant made to Mr Patterson is in accordance with the terms of his service agreement dated 1 July 2025.
Notifications have been made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation and are set out below.

14 January 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Update on Energy Charter Treaty Claim
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to update investors in relation to the Company's ICSID registered Energy Charter Treaty claim against the Republic of Slovenia. Further to the Company's announcement on 23 September 2025, the Company has received an update from the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes which confirms that the Arbitration Tribunal has made good progress with the draft award and are still in deliberation. The Company still expects that the Tribunal's award on merits will be issued towards the end of Q1 2026. Further updates will be announced as necessary.

latest.title: Option Agreement for exploitation in Utah
latest.content:
26 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 9724I
Ascent Resources PLC
26 November 2025
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN THE UNITED STATES, CANADA, JAPAN, SOUTH AFRICA OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL. ANY FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THESE RESTRICTIONS MAY CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF APPLICABLE SECURITIES LAWS. PLEASE SEE THE SECTION ENTITLED "IMPORTANT INFORMATION" TOWARDS THE END OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
26 November 2025
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Option Agreement for the exploitation of critical mineral-rich brines in Utah
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to announce that it has, together with its partner American Helium, LLC ("
AH
"), entered into an option agreement ("
Option Agreement
") with Neometals Ltd (ASX: NMT) and its partner Omaha Value, LLC (together
"NMT/OMA"
).
The agreement grants NMT/OMA a 60-day exclusive option (extendable by mutual consent) to negotiate a definitive access and use licence over Ascent and AH's portfolio of existing oil and gas wells and leases in the Paradox Basin, Utah, for the purpose of exploring and potentially extracting lithium and potash from critical mineral-rich brines.
Key benefits of the proposed arrangement
·
Represents a new monetisation pathway for Ascent's large Paradox Basin acreage position with no upfront drilling or development cost to the Company;
·
Provides access to deep, artesian brines previously intersected during historic oil, gas and helium exploration;
·
Leverages the critical mineral status of lithium and potash, which may qualify for accelerated permitting and potential US federal funding;
·
Utilises existing wellbores and infrastructure, significantly reducing time and cost versus drilling new wells.
Financial terms of the Option Agreement
The following terms are gross and corresponding benefits to be shared between Ascent and AH pro-rata of their respective interests in the portfolio, i.e. Ascent 49% and American Helium 51%.
·
Exclusivity fee: US$50,000 (payable within five business days);
·
Option exercise fee (if exercised): US$50,000;
·
Permitting fee: US$1.9 million payable on grant of relevant mineral leases, and pro-rated if fewer wells are available;
·
Annual licence fee: US$200,000 (payable in arrears);
·
Royalty on future brine production: 2.5% of gross revenue, increasing to 3.5% if commercial extraction has not commenced within five years of mineral lease grant.
Strategic rationale
This transaction highlights the embedded multi-commodity potential of Ascent's Utah and Colorado acreage across hydrocarbons, helium, CO2 sequestration and now critical mineral-rich brines. By partnering with an experienced lithium-focused developer in Neometals and a well-resourced local partner in Omaha Value, Ascent can unlock additional value from its existing asset base at minimal cost and dilution to shareholders.
Next steps
NMT/OMA will now undertake technical, legal and commercial due diligence during the 60-day exclusivity period. The Company will update the market on material developments as appropriate.
There is no certainty that the option will be exercised or that binding definitive agreements will be entered into.
Enquiries
Ascent Resources plc
Jean-Michel Doublet
info3@ascentresources.co.uk
Zeus, Nominated Adviser & Broker
James Joyce / James Bavister
0203 829 5000
Novum Securities, Joint Broker

company_30s.what_they_do: AST.L is a specialist dispute‑resolution firm that handles cross‑border arbitration and litigation, with a focus on energy‑sector and treaty‑based claims. The company represents large corporates, governments and multinationals in complex legal battles that often span several jurisdictions.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The firm occupies a niche market where expertise in energy charter treaties and international arbitration can command premium fees. Its clients are high‑profile and its cases often involve significant sums, giving it a steady stream of potential revenue and a reputation that can drive future work.
company_30s.current_state: AST.L has been in a deep trough, with its shares heavily discounted after a series of share‑option grants and a lack of fresh capital. The company is currently focused on managing its existing disputes and has not yet released a new round of financial results, indicating a period of low liquidity and high cash burn.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy issuing large batches of share options to directors, which signals an attempt to retain talent but also raises concerns about dilution.

A series of arbitration updates—particularly the Slovenia case and the Energy Charter Treaty claim—shows that the firm is still fighting high‑stakes disputes that could either resolve favorably or drag on.

Regular financial disclosures (interim results, AGM updates, board changes) indicate that the company is maintaining corporate governance, but the lack of a recent earnings announcement keeps the market in a holding pattern.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter; the community is largely silent, which is typical for a contrarian play.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some traders worry that the massive option grants will dilute the remaining shareholders and that the firm’s ongoing litigation could fail, leaving the company without a clear revenue stream.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from neutral to mildly negative as more share‑option announcements came to light, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent spikes, meaning the market is not currently tracking AST.L. This silence heightens the potential for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A settlement or favorable ruling in the Slovenia arbitration could inject fresh capital and credibility.
A positive outcome on the Energy Charter Treaty claim would unlock a large payment stream.
The upcoming Q1 2026 earnings release could reveal a turnaround in cash flow and a potential refinancing plan.
Any announcement of a strategic partnership or new client contract would signal business momentum.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm the thesis are: (1) a press release announcing a settlement or win, (2) a jump in trading volume above the 50‑day average, and (3) a price rebound to at least 70% of the all‑time high.
expect_next.timing_regime: AST.L is in the early phase of a deep crash. The stock has already capitulated, but it is still waiting for a catalyst to kick off mean reversion. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so a breakout could be swift once a trigger arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The shares are 86% below their peak, and if the firm’s disputes resolve favorably, the price could rise to roughly the midpoint of that range—an upside of about 260%. The historical record for deep‑crash setups shows that a rebound to 50% of the all‑time high can generate multi‑hundred percent gains, matching the performance of the RED and ORANGE signal categories.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Dilution from the large option grants could erode shareholder value if the firm fails to secure a win. Ongoing litigation carries the risk of a costly defeat or a protracted settlement, which would keep the stock depressed. A worst‑case scenario is a further decline to the current trough, meaning a loss of roughly 86% of the peak.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside potential ~260% versus downside risk of ~86% of the peak—an asymmetry that favors a contrarian long if a catalyst materialises.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce above the 70% support level, size the position at 10% of discretionary capital, and set a stop 10% below that bounce. Move the stop to breakeven once the price gains 5% and trail it by 3% thereafter.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a settlement announcement, a new client contract, or a Q1 earnings release shows a positive cash‑flow outlook. Trigger a trade if the price recovers to 70% of the all‑time high and volume spikes above the 50‑day average.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a major dilution event (e.g., a new round of share‑option grants that pushes the share price below 20% of the peak) or if litigation outcomes are announced as defeats, consider exiting or avoiding the position.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AST.L sits at an 86% crash; a settlement or favorable arbitration outcome could spark a mean‑reversion to the midpoint, offering a 260% upside against an 86% downside—an asymmetrical contrarian play that warrants a watchful eye for a catalyst.
